sections.paragraph:
ἀμεριμνία, ας, ἡ (μέριμνα ‘solicitude’, μεριμνάω ‘care for’; Plut., Mor. 830a; Appian, Liby. 65 §290; Secundus [II a.d.], Sententiae 8b of a wife ἀμεριμνίας ἐμπόδιον ‘an impediment to freedom fr. care’; Herodian 2, 4, 6; ins and pap in many mngs., incl. t.t.) freedom from care = confidence (Appian, Syr. 61 §321; Jos., Bell. 1, 627.—Ps 107:10 Sym. has ἀ. for ἐλπίς LXX) ἐν ἀ. θεοῦ w. God-given freedom fr. care IPol 7:1.—DELG s.v. μέριμνα.

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Secundus Secundus , Athenian phil., Sententiae, II a.d.—List 5

  1. fr. = from

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

incl. incl. = including

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

Syr. Syr. = Syriac

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 53
